---
title: "Network Analysis"
---

<span class="section-badge advanced">Advanced Topics</span>

::: {.coming-soon}
## 🚧

**This page is under construction.**

Content covering psychometric network analysis will be added here.
:::

---

## What this page will cover

Psychometric network analysis conceptualises psychological constructs as **networks of interacting components** rather than as reflections of a single latent variable. For example, depression might be modelled as a network of interrelated symptoms (insomnia, fatigue, anhedonia, etc.) rather than as a single underlying factor.

Key methods:

- **GGM (Gaussian Graphical Model)**: estimates partial correlations between variables, with edges representing direct relationships after controlling for all other variables
- **EBIC graphical LASSO**: a regularisation method to estimate sparse networks and avoid over-fitting
- **Centrality measures**: which nodes (variables) are most central to the network? (strength, betweenness, closeness)
- **Community detection**: identifying clusters of closely connected nodes

JASP has a Network Analysis module.

---

## Useful resources in the meantime

- Borsboom, D., & Cramer, A. O. J. (2013). Network analysis: An integrative approach to the structure of psychopathology. *Annual Review of Clinical Psychology*, 9, 91–121.
- Epskamp, S., & Fried, E. I. (2018). A tutorial on regularized partial correlation networks. *Psychological Methods*, 23(4), 617–634.
- The **qgraph** R package: [cran.r-project.org/package=qgraph](https://cran.r-project.org/package=qgraph)
